text: At the boutique, I bought the cheaper jacket.
Now I want to visit the museum in the city center.

Excuse me, how do I get to the museum?
Take the subway and get off at the next station. Then go up to the street.
And then? Where is the museum?
Go straight and turn left at the corner. The museum is on your left, next to a cafe.
Is the bus stop near the museum too?
Yes, it is across from the museum. Look at this map. Have a nice day!
Excuse me, where is the station?
Is it far? Can I walk there?

Good morning, and welcome aboard the Paris metro. This train goes to the city center. The next station is Louvre. To visit the museum, get off at the next stop and take exit number two. After the museum, if you want to see the Eiffel Tower, take the bus near the station. The bus stop is on your left, across from the cafe. Go straight for two minutes and you will see it. The ticket for the bus is the same as the metro ticket, so keep your ticket with you. If you are lost, look at the map on the wall, or ask a local for directions. Have a wonderful day in our beautiful city.

Today I went around Paris by myself, and I used only English to find my way. First, I took the subway to the city center. I asked a local, how do I get to the museum, and he told me to get off at the next station. Then I looked at the map and walked straight for two minutes. The museum was on my left, next to a small cafe. After that, I wanted to see the Eiffel Tower, so I found the bus stop across from the station. I bought a ticket and took the bus. The driver told me to get off at the third stop and turn right at the corner. I was a little nervous, but I did not get lost. I felt so proud, because I found my way in a big city in English.

First, you look at the map to find the place. Next, you take the subway or the bus to the city center. After that, you get off at the right station. Then, you ask a local how to get to the place. Finally, you go straight and turn left or right at the corner.
